hallucinated.text: For placing an order, we should streamline the process so that customers can add items to their cart, proceed to checkout with minimal steps, and complete payment without too many interactions.

Similarly, checking order status should only require logging in or entering an order number, then viewing the current status page which displays all relevant information like delivery date and tracking details.
For example, when a customer logs in and starts a new order, it should recognize them and auto-populate fields like shipping address and payment method where applicable.
Ideally, it would also allow us to show any saved payment details securely so they can just confirm or change if needed.
It should be able to manage a list of items across different orders, so that if they start one order but then want to make another, they can easily switch between them without losing any items.
For example, when reading long articles or filling out forms, being able to increase the text size can make it much easier on the eyes and improve readability.
We should ensure that when users navigate through different sections of the application, they don't encounter issues like broken back button functionality or bookmarking problems.
Currently, the system can be a bit cumbersome because it has too many links that make the text hard to read at a glance.
We need to ensure that users can easily find help when they need it.
We need to ensure that users can easily understand where they are in the application.
For example, when a user is filling out a form for creating an account, it should clearly indicate which step they're on and how many steps remain.
For instance, screen readers should be able to navigate through the application smoothly without any issues.

omitted.text: Operating Environment Web Store shall operate with the following internet browsers: Microsoft Internet Explorer version 6 and 7, Netscape Communicator Version 4 and 5.
Web Store shall operate on an Intel based system with Slackware Linux 2.6 and Apache Web Server.
User Documentation Shall install online help for users via the web interface Shall deliver Operations and Maintenance manual, Users Guide book, and Installation Instructions for the Administrator.

SI-1.3: Plug-ins interface Communications Interfaces CI-1: The WebOrder system shall send an e-mail confirmation to the customer that the items they ordered will be delivered to the shipping address along with tracking number.
Quality Attribute Requirements Upon the USB being plugged in the system shall be able to be deployed and operational in less than 1 minute.
The system shall be able to handle 1000 customers logged in concurrently at the same time.
The system shall be able to validate credit card in less than 2 seconds.
Security Requirements The system shall validate credit cards against fraud.
The system shall encrypt all sensitive information via https.
The system shall encrypt all customer data in database.
The system shall be protected by open source firewall called Firestarter.
http://www.fs-security.com/ Availability Requirements The system shall have an availability of 99.99%.

hallucinated.text: It should be able to handle different types of products and quantities.
For both customers and vendors, these emails should be triggered under specific conditions such as order confirmations, payment reminders, account updates, and support requests.
We've had issues with slow login times, so we need the system to authenticate users efficiently, ensuring that it never takes more than 5 seconds for a user to log in.
Additionally, the system must support multiple browsers including Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge on both Windows and macOS operating systems.
The user interface should display product titles and metadata prominently but never show any private information like credit card numbers or passwords in the browser interface.
Only authorized users, identified by their unique login credentials, should have access to certain administrative features.
We must not allow unauthorized users to modify or view sensitive data.
Lastly, we need robust logging and auditing capabilities for all user activities, ensuring that every action is recorded and can be traced back to a specific user.
Their accounts must already be registered before they can log in.
When they log in, the system should allow them to access their order history but never let them modify or delete orders that aren't theirs.
It's important because we want to protect user data and ensure only authorized users can make changes.
Additionally, before performing any maintenance tasks, such as software updates or server checks, the system should automatically back up all critical data.

omitted.text: DOCUMENT OVERVIEW This document outlines the software capabilities required for the XS components for the DII COE.
COE sponsors shall adhere to compliance level requirements described in the I&RTS.

In the fixed (static) mode of operation (base or data processing megacenter), the data management services shall have the capability of being tuned by on-site personnel to adjust for varying workloads and sizes of associated databases.
The XS shall have the ability to redefine or reset names of connect descriptors to database server instances.
At a minimum, the session time-out values shall be user definable and be able to be reset prior to initialization of a user session.
End User Mode: Portion of XS services shall be used by various classes of users: data consumers, data and database managers, network information infrastructure resource managers.
Some of these uses of the data management services will entail unique requirements that shall be fulfilled within the capability of XS services.
In addition, maintenance also shall pertain to shutdown, open not-mounted and online/off-line implementations, modifications, upgrade, or other related actions.
The data management services shall support managing various types of data, database architectures and platforms that includes hardware and software at the specified sites.
In support of training activities, the data management services shall provide for the same processing as would be encountered in a production environment.
DATA ACCESS SERVICES INTERNAL INTERFACE REQUIREMENTS The XS function shall provide standard APIs as specified in Appendix C.
The XS shall internally interface (transparent to the operator) with the existing data elements of the DBIF, DAC, and COTS RDBMS products.

hallucinated.text: The software should allow easy updates and debugging for smooth operation under various conditions.
The system should handle up to 1000 concurrent users without issues.
The software must support automated testing across all environments to catch issues early.
The system should be designed with clear architecture and modular components.
The software should be able to run on multiple platforms without significant modifications.
The system should validate credit card information for returning users without requiring re-entry.
The software should handle different types of user roles and permissions without conflicts.
The system should be able to scale efficiently and maintain performance under heavy load.
The software must ensure data encryption both in transit and at rest.
The system should be easily ported to other platforms if needed.
The software should handle different environments, including various browsers.
The system should be designed with a robust logging mechanism for debugging and auditing purposes.

hallucinated.text: The Salesman master should track the product or service being sold, including its ID and description during a sale.
The Salesman master should record the quantity sold during a sale.
The Salesman master should capture the price per unit and total sale amount during a sale.
The Salesman master should record payment method information for each transaction.
The Salesman master should log the date and time of the sale, along with any special notes or comments from the Salesman.

The system should log IP address and timestamp for each entry to maintain audit trails. Regarding sales information, we only need to capture basic transaction data like order ID and total amount, but not any personal details.
Cash is the only mode accepted during distribution transactions.
